name: environment_clinical_trial_amendment_would_harbor_st_lt
premise: A Environment field incident unit is in a fast-moving incident involving environment clinical trial amendment would harbor st lt. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases ecological harm, community impact, and compliance. Stakeholders are already framing this as short term vs long term under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Soren Haddad
agents.identity: I am Soren Haddad, accountable for a defensible call in Environment when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays long term for short term.

agents.name: Talia Kaur
agents.identity: I am Talia Kaur, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Environment.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so long term isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of short term.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect long term.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Soren Haddad must decide under short term vs long term. What does Soren Haddad do?
